title: USCIS clarifies foreign national registration requirement; Who it applies to
description: USCIS has finalized a new online registration and biometrics rule for certain foreign nationals in the US. This policy, effective since April 2025, targets those lacking standard immigration records. Individuals entering without inspection, certain visa-exempt Canadians, and 14-year-olds in the US must register. The agency assures no new groups are affected, emphasizing procedural updates and traveler-friendly changes to an existing system.

Tensions flare at Manipur border as security forces stop Kuki march over Naga economic blockade
description: Tension flared on the Imphal West-Kangpokpi border as Kukis attempted to march towards Namdilong, protesting a prolonged economic blockade by Naga groups. Security forces intervened, preventing the march. The blockade, enforced by Naga organizations seeking justice for six slain civilians, has severely impacted essential supplies to Kangpokpi. Both communities remain on edge amidst escalating demands for action and justice.

Born in the USA: Supreme Court rebuffs Trump's bid to end birthright citizenship
description: The US Supreme Court has delivered a significant blow to President Trump's executive order, upholding birthright citizenship for children born on American soil to undocumented or temporary visa holders. In a 6-3 decision, the court affirmed that the 14th Amendment guarantees citizenship to nearly all individuals born in the U.S., reaffirming over a century of legal precedent. This ruling removes a key immigration initiative from the administration's agenda.

description: Dr. Vikas Satwik, a Bengaluru-based neonatologist, finds running essential for his demanding profession. Inspired by charity marathons in the UK, he now runs weekly to maintain physical and mental readiness for unpredictable emergencies. His participation in a 10K run supports the Neonatal Foundation of India, an organization he credits with significantly improving newborn care outcomes and lives. Dr.

description: BJP state president Samik Bhattacharya has strongly condemned the act of throwing eggs, stating it's not the party's culture and urging members to refrain from such actions that could damage the party's national image. He also cautioned against arrogance following recent electoral success, emphasizing that the public rejected the TMC's hubris. Bhattacharya further called for an investigation into government spending on lawyers.

Can Zohran Mamdani become US president? Here's what he said
description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's political influence is growing, but a presidential bid remains out of reach. Despite meeting the age and residency requirements, the Constitution's natural-born citizen clause disqualifies him, as he was born in Uganda. Mamdani, however, expressed contentment with the current constitutional framework, emphasizing his focus on New York City.

40°C and counting: Europe was built to beat the cold. Now heatwave is exposing its biggest weakness
description: Europe, once crafted to stay warm during harsh winters, now grapples with a grim new challenge: relentless heatwaves. Buildings that sheltered against frosty temperatures have turned into sweltering traps, revealing a serious flaw in adapting to intense summer heat. With infrastructure buckling under record temperatures and a surge in heat-related fatalities, a fundamental reevaluation of urban planning is desperately needed to face an increasingly hotter reality.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
description: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has exited New York City real estate at a steep loss, selling his stake in struggling landlord A&E Real Estate for just six cents on the dollar. The Bloomberg-reported sale lands as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze on one million rent-stabilized apartments squeezes landlords already battered by 2019 rent laws, soaring costs and mounting unpaid rent.

'994 days of genocide': Microsoft engineer quits, emails thousands on his final day
description: A Microsoft data center technician in Italy resigned on his final day, emailing thousands of colleagues across Europe to accuse the company of powering Israel's war in Gaza through its Azure cloud and European data centers. Published by No Azure for Apartheid, the email cites the Unit 8200 surveillance exposé and urges Microsoft workers to "reclaim our labor" and divest.
